articles.title: LACK AI SKILLS
articles.summary: Are designers afraid of AI taking their jobs? No - but many of them aren't sure where to start.

articles.title: Get the full report by Miro →
LEAKER SHARES ALLEGED IPHONE 17 TECHWOVEN CASE COLORS
articles.summary: Apple is rumored to replace its failed FineWoven cases with new “TechWoven” ones that feature a more technical look. Leaker Majin Bu has shared images showing five colors: orange, purple, green, blue, and gray. Close-ups reveal small dings on both blue and black cases, raising concerns about durability.

articles.title: AI WEBSITE BUILDER LOVABLE INCREASINGLY ABUSED FOR MALICIOUS ACTIVITY
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are increasingly exploiting Lovable, an AI-powered website builder, to create phishing pages and malware-dropping sites that impersonate major brands. Proofpoint researchers identified tens of thousands of malicious Lovable URLs since February, including campaigns targeting Microsoft credentials, UPS payment fraud, cryptocurrency theft, and zgRAT malware distribution. While Lovable has implemented real-time detection systems and claims to have blocked around 1,000 violating projects, security researchers confirmed the platform can still be used to generate fraudulent sites.

articles.title: QWEN-IMAGE EDIT GIVES PHOTOSHOP A RUN FOR ITS MONEY WITH AI-POWERED TEXT-TO-IMAGE EDITS THAT WORK IN SECONDS
articles.summary: Qwen-Image Edit is an open-source AI model that performs Photoshop-like image editing tasks using simple text instructions in seconds. Built on a 20-billion-parameter foundation model, it handles both semantic transformations and precise appearance changes while supporting bilingual text editing in English and Chinese. The model is available across multiple platforms. API access starts at $0.045 per image, with a free quota of 100 images valid for 180 days after account activation.

articles.title: THE PSYCHOLOGY OF COLOR IN UX DESIGN AND DIGITAL PRODUCTS
articles.summary: A Google designer explores how color functions as a strategic tool in digital design, influencing user behavior through psychological associations while reinforcing brand identity and creating emotional connections. The piece outlines the HSB color model and explains how different hues carry cultural meanings that can guide user perception and decision-making in digital products. Key considerations include building accessible color systems that meet WCAG contrast requirements, creating scalable palettes that work across touchpoints, and maintaining consistency to establish brand recognition and user trust.

articles.title: VIBE CODING MAKES PROTOTYPING CLOSE TO CODE, CLOSER TO USERS
articles.summary: Vibe coding transforms prototyping by allowing designers to express interaction goals in natural language while AI translates them into working code and functional prototypes. This approach enables designers to prototype closer to real applications rather than static mockups, creating immediate feedback loops and bringing accessibility considerations into the process much earlier. The method has become essential for rapid innovation, deeper cross-disciplinary collaboration, and building more inclusive experiences that reflect real-world complexity.

articles.title: WHY USE DESIGN TOKENS? SEVEN PRACTICAL REASONS DESIGNERS AND DEVELOPERS SWEAR BY
articles.summary: Design tokens are named, reusable variables that store visual design attributes like colors and typography, replacing hardcoded values with self-explanatory names for consistency across digital products. They offer key benefits: ensuring design consistency across platforms, speeding up workflows through automation, enabling seamless rebranding and theming, bridging designer-developer communication gaps, future-proofing design systems, reducing human error and technical debt, and powering advanced automation tools. Unlike static style guides, tokens update instantly across all platforms when changed in one central location, making them essential for scalable design systems.

articles.title: DESIGN SPELLS
articles.summary: Small, often-overlooked design details like micro-interactions and Easter eggs bring personality and joy to the web. Design Spells was created to celebrate this hidden magic.

articles.title: UI ROOT
articles.summary: Tools and resources that supercharge your UI/UX workflow.

articles.title: AI DESIGN EDITOR
articles.summary: Design stunning graphics in seconds, powered by AI, guided by you.

articles.title: ONLY BALANCES HERITAGE WITH CULTURE IN CAMBRIDGE AUDIO'S SOPHISTICATED REBRAND
articles.summary: Cambridge's new brand platform, Made by Music, draws from its music-driven culture to position the company at the center of contemporary music life through events, partnerships, and culturally relevant storytelling. The refreshed identity uses bold typography, a flexible logotype and marque system, and a restrained yet expressive color palette to balance premium design with cultural energy.

articles.title: FROM SKIM TO SUBSTANCE: DESIGNING THE FUTURE OF READING
articles.summary: Benjamin Cain criticizes “skimmable texts” as shallow and fake, but this response argues skimming is a real adaptation to fragmented attention and time scarcity in the digital age. Instead of rejecting it, writers and designers should treat skimming as the entry point, using curation and layered formats to guide readers from quick scans into depth, turning a glance into lasting engagement.

articles.title: YELLOW, PURPLE, AND THE MYTH OF “ACCESSIBILITY LIMITS COLOR PALETTES”
articles.summary: The myth that accessibility requirements severely limit color palette choices is debunked through six WCAG-compliant yellow and purple color palettes that work in both light and dark modes. A detailed Figma tutorial demonstrates how to create accessible palettes by testing contrast ratios and documenting safe color combinations. Accessibility enhances rather than restricts creativity, providing designers with practical tools for building inclusive color systems.

articles.title: PIZZA HUT'S NEW LOGO IS BAKED TO PERFECTION
articles.summary: Pizza Hut has unveiled a new logo for the UK, Canada, and select markets that ditches the round pizza-evoking background for italicized lettering with decorative flourishes.

articles.title: UNIVERSAL DESIGN VS INCLUSIVE DESIGN
articles.summary: Universal design focuses on creating products usable by everyone without requiring customization or adaptation.
